name: Community Organizing
definition: Bringing people together to act in shared self-interest and build relational power.
mechanism: Transforms individual grievances into collective relational power.
successFactors: Deep listening, indigenous leadership, winnable demands.

name: Mutual Aid
definition: Voluntary, reciprocal exchange of resources based on solidarity.
mechanism: Meets survival needs while exposing state failures.

name: Nonviolent Direct Action
definition: Strategic physical presence to disrupt injustice without violence.
mechanism: Creates an unignorable crisis.

name: Boycott & Divestment
definition: Coordinated economic withdrawal to force change.
mechanism: Hits the financial bottom line.

name: Strategic Campaigning
definition: Planned activities toward a specific goal in a timeframe.
mechanism: Focuses diffuse energy onto a target.

name: Narrative Influence
definition: Using storytelling and media to shift cultural assumptions.
mechanism: Changes 'cultural common sense'.

name: Coalition Building
definition: Alliances between groups to amplify power.
mechanism: Aggregates power, demonstrates broad support.

name: Participatory Democracy
definition: Direct citizen input into decisions.
mechanism: Redistributes institutional power directly.

name: Civic Education
definition: Pedagogy connecting personal struggles to systemic forces.
mechanism: Breaks internalized oppression.

name: Local Institution Building
definition: Creating independent, prefigurative institutions.
mechanism: Builds dual power.

name: Policy Advocacy
definition: Lobbying driven by impacted communities.
mechanism: Translates street heat into codified law.

name: Labor Action & Strikes
definition: Coordinated withdrawal of labor.
mechanism: Directly targets economic engines.
bestContext: Workers have leverage over choke points.

name: Open Source Commons
definition: Building/distributing resources freely to bypass monopolies.
mechanism: Renders oppressive monopolies obsolete.

name: Transformative Justice
definition: Community-led accountability replacing police.
mechanism: Shifts from punishment to root-cause healing.

name: Whistleblowing
definition: Exposing hidden documents from bottom up.
mechanism: Breaks information asymmetry.

name: Strategic Litigation
definition: Using courts for sweeping precedent.
mechanism: Leverages state's legal architecture.
successFactors: Excellent legal teams, broad movement backing.

name: Debt & Rent Strikes
definition: Collective refusal to pay exploitative debts.
mechanism: Turns individual shame into collective financial leverage.

name: Digital Resistance
definition: Using cyber tactics to protect/disrupt.
mechanism: Exploits reliance on digital infrastructure.

name: Artivism & Culture
definition: Merging art with activism to subvert narratives.
mechanism: Bypasses logic to hit emotional truth.

name: Blockadia
definition: Sustained physical occupation to protect space.
mechanism: Increases friction until project abandonment.

name: Sit-Ins & Occupations
definition: Taking physical control of a contested or symbolic space and refusing to leave.
example: Student occupations of university administration buildings.
mechanism: Halts normal operations and forces authorities to negotiate or use visible force.

name: Teach-Ins
definition: Uninterrupted, public educational forums, often held in disrupted spaces.
mechanism: Combines disruption with mass political education.
bestContext: When public understanding of an issue is low.

name: Tax Resistance
definition: Refusal to pay taxes to a government funding war or unjust policies.
mechanism: Directly starves the state machinery of resources.
weaknesses: Easily isolated if numbers are small.
bestContext: Unpopular wars or universally despised policies.

name: Work-to-Rule
definition: Workers doing no more than the exact minimum required by rules, slowing down production.
example: Air traffic controllers strictly following safety protocols to delay flights.
mechanism: Malicious compliance that breaks systems relying on informal worker goodwill.

name: Guerilla Gardening
definition: Cultivating land that organizers do not legally own, transforming blighted spaces.
example: Planting community gardens in abandoned urban lots.
mechanism: Prefigurative reclamation of the commons.

name: Alternative Media & Pirate Radio
definition: Creating independent broadcast networks to bypass state or corporate media.
mechanism: Breaks media monopolies to distribute truth and organizing logistics.

name: Election Boycott
definition: Mass organized refusal to participate in rigged or illegitimate elections.
example: Boycotts of sham referendums in autocracies.
mechanism: Denies the regime the mandate of democratic legitimacy.
risks: Ensures the opposition takes total control.
strengths: Exposes the farce of the system.

name: Hunger Strike
definition: Voluntary fasting to exert immense moral pressure on authorities.
mechanism: Weaponizes the state's responsibility for the striker's life.
risks: Death, permanent organ damage, forced feeding.
weaknesses: Can be ignored by ruthless opponents.

name: Mass Declarations
definition: Public, signed pledges of non-compliance or demand.
example: Manifesto of the 343 (abortion rights in France).
mechanism: Provides safety in numbers for admitting 'crimes' or holding controversial views.
risks: Creating a target list for authorities.

name: Shadow Parliaments
definition: Holding parallel democratic processes to contrast with corrupted official ones.
mechanism: Highlights the democratic deficit and builds a mandate for alternative leadership.
scaling: Can evolve into actual dual power.
bestContext: When official democratic avenues are closed.

name: Walkouts
definition: Sudden, coordinated exit from a workplace, school, or institution.
mechanism: Brief, high-visibility disruption and demonstration of numbers.

name: Flash Mobs
definition: Sudden assembly in a public place for a brief, coordinated action, followed by rapid dispersal.
mechanism: Exploits the element of surprise for viral media capture.

name: Divestment Campaigns
definition: Pressuring institutions to sell off assets tied to harmful industries.
mechanism: Removes social license and increases cost of capital for bad actors.

name: Corporate Camp-outs
definition: Sustained physical occupation outside corporate headquarters or shareholder meetings.
mechanism: Brings the physical reality of abstract corporate harm to the executives' doorstep.
strengths: Forces executives to face the public.

name: Fraternization
definition: Direct, empathetic engagement with the agents of repression (e.g., police/soldiers) to induce defection.
example: Otpor activists offering flowers to Serbian police.
mechanism: Breaks the psychological barrier between enforcer and citizen.
strengths: Can neutralize the state's monopoly on violence.
bestContext: Regime change scenarios where military loyalty wavers.

name: Sanctuary Spaces
definition: Declaring a space off-limits to specific state enforcement, providing refuge.
example: Churches providing sanctuary to undocumented immigrants.
mechanism: Leverages the moral authority of a space against the legal authority of the state.
successFactors: Strong moral backing, network of support.
bestContext: Targeting unjust immigration or fugitive laws.

name: Civil Disobedience
definition: The open, peaceful violation of a law to protest its injustice.
example: Rosa Parks refusing to give up her seat.
mechanism: Forces the state to arrest normal citizens, exposing the law's cruelty.
successFactors: Willingness to go to jail, legal strategy.

name: General Strike
definition: Cross-sector strike involving a critical mass of the entire workforce.
mechanism: Brings the entire economy to a halt.
scaling: The ultimate scale of labor action.

name: Reverse Strike
definition: Undertaking unauthorized public works to highlight government neglect.
example: Filling potholes or building community centers without permits.
mechanism: Shames the state by doing their job better.
weaknesses: Relies on volunteer labor and materials.

name: Overloading Systems
definition: Flooding administrative systems with legitimate requests or paperwork to freeze operations.
example: Mass filing of FOIA requests or contesting every minor ticket.
mechanism: Uses the bureaucracy's own rules against it.

name: Guerilla Theater
definition: Surprise theatrical performances in public spaces to satirize power.
mechanism: Uses humor and absurdity to break through political apathy.
bestContext: Overly serious or hypocritical political events.

name: Alternative Currencies
definition: Creating local or digital currencies and time banks to bypass the dominant financial system.
mechanism: Keeps wealth within the community and values un-monetized labor.

name: Mass Resignations
definition: Coordinated quitting by skilled workers or officials to protest ethical violations.
example: State Dept officials resigning over war policies.
mechanism: Strips the institution of its operational brain trust.
strengths: Severe reputational damage to the target.

name: Social Ostracism
definition: Total social boycott of individuals responsible for atrocities.
example: Refusing to serve, speak to, or acknowledge enforcers.
mechanism: Imposes massive psychological costs on perpetrators.
risks: Can devolve into vigilantism or target innocents.
bestContext: Small towns or insular communities facing occupation.

name: Subvertising
definition: Altering corporate billboards and advertisements to convey anti-capitalist or activist messages.
mechanism: Hijacks corporate visual space to broadcast dissent.
successFactors: Clever design matching the original ad.
strengths: High visibility, reclaims the mental environment.

name: Protest Emigration
definition: Mass, highly visible exodus from a regime to protest its conditions.
example: East Germans fleeing through Hungary in 1989.
mechanism: Brain drains the state and creates an international crisis.
weaknesses: Leaves the homeland to the oppressors.

name: Rolling Jubilees
definition: Raising funds to buy distressed debt on the secondary market and legally abolishing it.
mechanism: Uses the mechanics of debt trading for mass liberation.
risks: Treats symptoms rather than root financial rules.
scaling: Millions of dollars of debt cleared for pennies.
bestContext: Predatory medical or student debt markets.

name: Human Chains
definition: Thousands linking arms to connect geographic points or surround buildings.
mechanism: Massive visual demonstration of unity and scale.
risks: Vulnerable to police breaking the chain.
strengths: Incredible media optics, low risk per person.
bestContext: Independence movements or massive solidarity drives.

name: Civic Hackathons
definition: Rapid, collaborative coding events to build tools that solve civic problems or expose data.
mechanism: Harnesses tech talent to build public infrastructure bypassing slow government tech.

name: Ethical Purchasing
definition: Organized channeling of consumer funds only toward cooperatives or ethical businesses.
example: Fair Trade movement, Buy Black campaigns.
mechanism: Builds the economic power of alternative institutions.
strengths: Positive reinforcement rather than just boycott.
